message.content:
Test file: src/game-of-life.spec.ts
Failing test: "should bring a dead cell to life when it has exactly 3 live neighbors (reproduction)"
Expected behavior: a dead cell with exactly 3 live neighbors becomes alive in the next generation
Current error: Expected: [[1, 1], [1, 1]], Received: [[1, 1], [1, 0]] - the reproduction rule is missing
Implementation file: src/game-of-life.ts

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: Let me start by reading the current state of the test and implementation files.

Now I understand the issue. The current implementation only handles live cells - it checks if a cell is alive and has 2 or 3 neighbors. It does not handle the reproduction rule where a dead cell with exactly 3 live neighbors comes alive.

The minimal fix is to add the reproduction condition: if a dead cell has exactly 3 neighbors, it becomes alive.

content.text:
All 7 active tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:

**Implementation**: Added the reproduction rule - when a dead cell has exactly 3 live neighbors, it becomes alive in the next generation.

**Result**: All tests now pass (7 passing, 3 todo)

**Approach**: The minimal change was a single additional condition `|| (!alive && neighbors === 3)` in the existing if-statement. This is the only change demanded by the failing test - no restructuring, no new functions, no additional logic.

The modified file is `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-04_02-45-17_game-of-life-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_sonnet-4-6-no-thinking/src/game-of-life.ts`.

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
